name: Phoenix Lights 1997
description: The Phoenix Lights refers to a series of widely sighted aerial phenomena observed in the skies over Arizona and Nevada, USA, on March 13, 1997. The events involved two distinct phenomena: a triangular formation of lights seen moving slowly over the state, and a series of stationary lights that appeared later near Phoenix. Proponents claim the lights were evidence of a massive, silent, solid craft of extraterrestrial origin, possibly miles wide. They point to eyewitness accounts from thousands of individuals, including law enforcement and military personnel, as evidence. Some interpretations suggest government involvement and a cover-up. Mainstream explanations attribute the triangular formation to flares dropped by A-10 Warthog aircraft during a military training exercise at the Barry M. Goldwater Range. The stationary lights are generally identified as flares dropped by the same aircraft. Critics argue that eyewitness accounts are subjective and unreliable, especially under conditions of darkness and heightened excitement. They emphasize the lack of verifiable physical evidence beyond the visual sightings. Skeptics also point to the psychological phenomenon of mass hysteria and the tendency for people to misinterpret ordinary events as extraordinary.
subtitle: Unidentified Aerial Phenomena over Arizona

claims.claim: A massive, solid, silent craft of extraterrestrial origin flew over Phoenix.
claims.evidence: Thousands of eyewitness accounts describing a large, dark, triangular object with lights.
claims.criticism: Eyewitness testimony is subjective and prone to misinterpretation. No verifiable physical evidence supports the existence of such a craft.

claims.claim: The U.S. military is covering up the true nature of the Phoenix Lights.
claims.evidence: Initial military denials followed by a later explanation involving flares, fueling suspicion of a cover-up.
claims.criticism: The military's explanation involving flares is consistent with available data and the timing of the sightings. No concrete evidence of a cover-up has been presented.

claims.claim: The Phoenix Lights represent a coordinated display of alien technology.
claims.evidence: The distinct events (triangular formation and stationary lights) suggest a deliberate and complex maneuver.
claims.criticism: The two events are likely unrelated, with the triangular formation explained by flares and the stationary lights also attributed to flares. There is no evidence of a coordinated display.

keyFigures.name: Lynne Kitei
keyFigures.role: Physician and proponent who documented the event and produced a documentary.

keyFigures.name: James McGaha
keyFigures.role: Retired Air Force pilot and astronomer who investigated the event and concluded it was flares.

mythologyConnections: Possible interpretations drawing parallels to sightings of celestial beings or signs in the sky in various mythologies.

timeline.event: Mass sightings of the Phoenix Lights occur.
Ongoing debate and investigation into the nature of the Phoenix Lights.

sources.author: Lynne Kitei
sources.work: The Phoenix Lights...We Are Not Alone
sources.title: Lynne Kitei

sources.author: Randall Cerveny
